r600: revise vkpeak RCA — SFN handles compute intrinsics natively

3 RCA agents completed and REFUTED the original hypothesis that
nir_lower_compute_system_values was missing. Key findings:

- r600 Gallium does NOT use nir_lower_compute_system_values for compute
- SFN ComputeShader (sfn_shader_cs.cpp) pins hardware registers:
  selector 0 = TID (local_invocation_id), selector 1 = TGID (workgroup_id)
- SPI_COMPUTE_INPUT_CNTL (0x286E8, NOT 0x286E0) = 0x7 enables delivery
- Info buffer at offset 0 (workgroup_size) and offset 16 (num_workgroups)

Revised most-likely vkpeak failure: Terakan SFN fork missing ComputeShader
class → compute intrinsics hit 'Unsupported instruction' at compile time.

commit.message:
Feature/corpus normalization v2 (#30)

* [CORPUS] Manual PR ingestion: hydrator, normalizer, CLI command

- GitHubRestHydrator (IPullRequestHydrator): fetches PR details, files, review
  comments, commits, and diff concurrently via GitHub REST API; saves raw
  snapshots (pr.json, files.json, review-comments.json) to fixture raw/ dir;
  maps responses to domain models; uses GITHUB_TOKEN env var for auth
- GitHubApiModels: internal DTOs for GitHub REST API response deserialization
- ManualSeedProvider (IDiscoveryProvider): converts static PR URL list to
  PullRequestCandidates; GetProviderName=manual, no incremental sync
- FixtureNormalizer: deterministic HydratedPullRequest -> FixtureMetadata;
  infers tags (async, contract-change, early-return, null-safety, state-mutation,
  logging, exception-flow, api-change) from diff content; detects dominant
  language from changed files; defaults tier to Discovery
- CorpusCommand + add-pr subcommand: 'gauntletci corpus add-pr --url <url>'
  hydrates a PR, normalizes it, saves metadata.json + SQLite index entry, and
  prints fixture ID and tags; --db and --fixtures flags for path overrides
- GauntletCI.Cli now references GauntletCI.Corpus
- corpus subcommand registered in Program.cs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* [CORPUS] Normalization pipeline + corpus normalize CLI command

NormalizationPipeline (Normalization/NormalizationPipeline.cs):
- NormalizeAsync: HydratedPullRequest -> metadata.json + diff.patch +
  expected.json (empty for discovery) + notes.md template + SQLite upsert
- ReNormalizeFromRawAsync: rebuilds HydratedPullRequest from existing raw/
  snapshots on disk (no GitHub API call) then re-runs full normalization
- Idempotent and resumable per spec

CorpusCommand (CLI):
- add-pr now uses NormalizationPipeline (writes diff.patch + expected.json)
- New normalize subcommand: 'gauntletci corpus normalize --fixture <id>'
  infers owner/repo/pr from fixture ID naming convention; supports --tier,
  --owner, --repo, --pr overrides; re-normalizes from raw/ without refetching
- Shared BuildPipeline helper wires CorpusDb + FixtureFolderStore + pipeline
- PrintMetadata helper shows tier, size, language, tags, next step hint

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feature(CORPUS): address PR #25 review comments

FixtureNormalizer:
- Add 'tier' parameter to Normalize() — was always hard-coded to Discovery,
  causing silver/gold re-normalization to demote fixtures to the wrong tier

NormalizationPipeline:
- Remove duplicate _fixturesBasePath field; use _store.BasePath as single
  source of truth to eliminate divergence risk
- Thread tier through NormalizeAsync() and ReNormalizeFromRawAsync()
- Add File.Exists checks for files.json and review-comments.json before
  reading, with descriptive FileNotFoundException on missing path
- Preserve original hydration timestamp during re-normalization to keep
  metadata.json idempotent across runs (reads CreatedAtUtc from existing
  metadata.json; falls back to DateTime.UtcNow only on first normalize)

GitHubRestHydrator:
- Implement IDisposable; CreateDefault sets ownsHttpClient=true so the
  internally-created HttpClient is properly disposed
- GetJsonListAsync: append ?per_page=100 to avoid silent truncation for
  large PRs (files, review-comments, commits endpoints)
- GetDiffAsync: clear Accept headers before adding the diff media type to
  prevent duplicate Accept header; add EnsureSuccessStatusCode() so 4xx/5xx
  bodies are not silently persisted as diff.patch
- ParsePrUrl: use int.TryParse with descriptive error on non-numeric PR segment

CorpusCommand:
- add-pr: use 'using var hydrator' for correct HttpClient disposal
- normalize: derive owner/repo/pr from existing metadata.json instead of
  splitting fixture ID on underscores (which is ambiguous for repos that
  contain underscores in their names)
- BuildPipeline: remove redundant fixturesPath arg to NormalizationPipeline
- PrintMetadata: fix hint from 'corpus run' (nonexistent) to 'corpus normalize'

ManualSeedProvider:
- Remove unused 'using GauntletCI.Corpus.Storage;'

commit.message:
feature(RULE): supersede GCI0030/GCI0033; absorb detection into GCI0024/GCI0016 (#29)

GCI0030 (DisposableResourceSafety) is now a hollow-shell rule - its
suffix-based disposable heuristic has been absorbed into GCI0024 (Resource
Lifecycle), which now handles both explicit type matching and suffix-based
detection (e.g. *Reader, *Connection, *Stream) via MatchDisposableType().

GCI0033 (AsyncSinkhole) is now a hollow-shell rule - its .Result/.Wait()
detection was a strict subset of GCI0016 (ConcurrencyAndStateRisk), which
already fires on the same patterns with the same guidance. Keeping both was
producing duplicate findings on every blocking async call.

Both superseded rules retain their class and ID to avoid breaking configs
or reflection-based discovery. All tests updated to verify:
- Superseded rules return empty findings
- Canonical rules (GCI0024, GCI0016) cover the previously-tested patterns

Added complementary cross-reference comments to GCI0023/GCI0029 clarifying
they check different dimensions (format vs. content) of log calls.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* [CORPUS] Manual PR ingestion: hydrator, normalizer, CLI command

- GitHubRestHydrator (IPullRequestHydrator): fetches PR details, files, review
  comments, commits, and diff concurrently via GitHub REST API; saves raw
  snapshots (pr.json, files.json, review-comments.json) to fixture raw/ dir;
  maps responses to domain models; uses GITHUB_TOKEN env var for auth
- GitHubApiModels: internal DTOs for GitHub REST API response deserialization
- ManualSeedProvider (IDiscoveryProvider): converts static PR URL list to
  PullRequestCandidates; GetProviderName=manual, no incremental sync
- FixtureNormalizer: deterministic HydratedPullRequest -> FixtureMetadata;
  infers tags (async, contract-change, early-return, null-safety, state-mutation,
  logging, exception-flow, api-change) from diff content; detects dominant
  language from changed files; defaults tier to Discovery
- CorpusCommand + add-pr subcommand: 'gauntletci corpus add-pr --url <url>'
  hydrates a PR, normalizes it, saves metadata.json + SQLite index entry, and
  prints fixture ID and tags; --db and --fixtures flags for path overrides
- GauntletCI.Cli now references GauntletCI.Corpus
- corpus subcommand registered in Program.cs

* [CORPUS] Normalization pipeline + corpus normalize CLI command

NormalizationPipeline (Normalization/NormalizationPipeline.cs):
- NormalizeAsync: HydratedPullRequest -> metadata.json + diff.patch +
  expected.json (empty for discovery) + notes.md template + SQLite upsert
- ReNormalizeFromRawAsync: rebuilds HydratedPullRequest from existing raw/
  snapshots on disk (no GitHub API call) then re-runs full normalization
- Idempotent and resumable per spec

CorpusCommand (CLI):
- add-pr now uses NormalizationPipeline (writes diff.patch + expected.json)

Two new read-only Kusto tools for query optimization and cluster awareness:

kusto_show_queryplan: Plans a query without executing it. Returns compact
output with planning stats (PlanSize, RelopSize), logical operator tree,
and execution hints (estimated_rows, concurrency, shard scan details with
filter detection). Useful for comparing query approaches, catching expensive
joins, and validating query syntax before execution.

kusto_diagnostics: Best-effort cluster health check running 7 diagnostic
commands (capacity, cluster, principal roles, diagnostics, workload groups,
rowstores, ingestion failures). Each section runs independently with
per-section error handling for mixed-permission environments.

* fix(#130,#131): fix markdown table column alignment + nested bullet rendering

Bug #130 — Table columns were misaligned because each Row measured its cells
independently; header and body cells in the same column had no shared parent
to enforce a consistent width.

Fix: replace the row-major layout (Column of Rows) with a column-major layout
(Row of Columns). Each logical column is now a Compose Column whose children
are the header cell and every data cell in that column. Compose naturally sizes
a Column to the widest child, so all cells in a column share the same width
without needing SubcomposeLayout, explicit width calculation, or weight
modifiers (which don't work inside a horizontalScroll container).
Modifier.fillMaxWidth() on every cell ensures uniform cell width within each
column. Outer Row border + clip preserved; per-cell border + padding unchanged.

Bug #131 — Second-level bullet items (e.g. '  * Sub-item') were rendered as
literal '*' text because the parser only matched top-level bullets with
BULLET_REGEX ('^[-*+]\s+(.*)') and indented variants fell through to the
paragraph accumulator.

Fix:
• Added INDENTED_BULLET_REGEX ('^([ \t]+)[-*+][ \t]+(.*)') to detect any
  list item with 1+ leading spaces/tabs followed by a marker.
• Added BulletItem(depth, text) data class; BulletList now stores
  List<BulletItem> instead of List<String>.
• Parser loop: top-level lines produce depth=0; indented lines map indent
  length to depth (2 spaces per level, tabs normalised to 4 spaces).
• Paragraph accumulator breaks on INDENTED_BULLET_REGEX so indented items
  are no longer absorbed into preceding paragraphs.
• BulletList Composable: applies Modifier.padding(start = 16.dp * depth) per
  item and renders '•' for depth 0 and '◦' for sub-levels.
• Added two new @Preview composables: MarkdownNestedBulletsPreview and
  MarkdownTableAlignmentPreview.

feat(types): wave14 TypedProgram boundary cert-3 — call_type_args output-contract validator (#940)

Add a dedicated fail-closed validator for the call_type_args checker->codegen
side-table boundary, closing the last unchecked output-contract gap in the
TypedProgram side-table family.

## What changed

admissibility.rs — Checker::validate_call_type_args_output_contract

  New associated function that prunes call_type_args at the output boundary
  under two fail-closed conditions:

  1. Orphaned span — the SpanKey is absent from the post-validation expr_types
     map.  Any span pruned by validate_expr_output_contract (leaked inference
     vars, cascading errors) is authoritative evidence the call-site entry is
     orphaned and must not reach codegen.  This mirrors the same contract
     already applied to method_call_receiver_kinds / method_call_rewrites.

  2. Leaked inference variable — any type argument still contains Ty::Var.
     Unresolved inference state must not cross the output boundary, per the
     Hew fail-closed / type-inference-boundary invariant.

  The previous inline retain in validate_checker_output_contract only covered
  condition (2); orphaned entries could survive if their span happened to be
  pruned by validate_expr_output_contract after call_type_args was populated.

  validate_checker_output_contract now delegates to the new function instead
  of the old single-condition retain.

## Tests added (admissibility.rs mod tests)

  - validate_call_type_args_output_contract_retains_valid_entries
  - validate_call_type_args_output_contract_prunes_orphaned_entries
  - validate_call_type_args_output_contract_prunes_leaked_inference_vars
  - validate_call_type_args_output_contract_mixed  (all three cases together)

## Validation

  cargo test -p hew-types: 523 lib + all integration tests — 0 failures

## Description

> ��� **This PR was authored by GitHub Copilot** as part of an automated
sync-over-async cleanup workflow.

Eliminates all sync-over-async anti-patterns in \StoreReaderTest.cs\ by
converting 6 test methods from synchronous (\ oid\) to asynchronous
(\sync Task\) and replacing \.Result\ blocking calls with proper
\wait\.

---

## Changes

| Method | \.Result\ removed | Notes |
|--------|-------------------|-------|
| \AddressCacheMockTest\ | 1 | \oid\  \sync Task\ |
| \TransportClientMockTest\ | 1 | \oid\  \sync Task\ |
| \StoreReaderBarrierTest\ | 4 | \oid\  \sync Task\ |
| \GlobalStrongConsistentWriteMockTest\ | 6 | \oid\  \sync Task\ |
| \GlobalStrongConsistencyMockTest\ | 5 | \ oid\ \sync Task\,
\AggregateException\ \GoneException\ |
| \TestWhenNRegionSynchronousCommitEnabledThenDoBarrierHead\ | 4 | \
oid\ \sync Task\, \AggregateException\ \DocumentClientException\ |

**Total: 21 sync-over-async calls eliminated**

## Why
- \.Result\ blocks the calling thread and risks deadlocks under a
\SynchronizationContext\
- \.Result\ wraps exceptions in \AggregateException\, hiding the real
failure type
- \wait\ properly unwraps exceptions, so catch blocks now use the
actual exception type

- No behavioral changes; only the async plumbing is updated

perf(maglev): inline Smi fast path for LoadContextSlot/StoreContextSlot

Replace full FFI stub calls with inline x86 memory access for the
common case where depth==0 context slots hold Smi values.

The fast path uses only non-allocated scratch registers (RDI, R11, RAX)
so no save/restore of live caller-saved registers is needed, eliminating
the push/pop overhead that dominated tight closure loops.

Fast path (~6 instructions):
  - Validate context is a raw pointer (SHR+CMP, rejects null/heap handles)
  - Dereference RefCell<JsContext> to get Vec<JsValue> data pointer
  - Check JsValue discriminant byte for Smi
  - MOVSXD the i32 payload to i64

Slow path (unchanged lean stub call) handles non-Smi values and
non-raw-pointer contexts.

For StoreContextSlot, an additional Smi check on the value avoids the
stub call when writing integer counters back to context slots.

* security: add table name whitelist validation to delete_child_rows

Implement defense-in-depth security by validating table names against
a compile-time whitelist before SQL interpolation. This prevents potential
SQL injection if the function is ever called with untrusted input.

- Add ALLOWED_TABLES const whitelist with 6 child tables
- Validate each table name before DELETE execution
- Document security rationale in function docstring
- All 147 indexer tests continue to pass
